task_name: Laparoscopic Bilateral Salpingo-Oophorectomy (BSO) - Surgical Planning and Anatomy Tutorial

frame_analyses.description: Stylized anatomical rendering of female reproductive system with vasculature highlighted in red and blue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 3D medical illustration - not a performance video

frame_analyses.description: Large pink 3D text reading '4D' with professional typography

Text overlay listing six surgical planning steps: Surgical Planning, Patient Positioning and Port Placement, Identifying the Ureters, Dissection of the Avascular Triangle, Division of Ligaments, Specimen Extraction
Detailed 3D anatomical model showing female pelvis with uterus, fallopian tubes, ovaries, and labeled vasculature (red arteries, blue veins)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Narration: 'The patient is a 40-year-old woman presenting for prophylactic laparoscopic bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy or BSO'

frame_analyses.description: 3D model of operating table showing patient positioning in dorsal lithotomy and Trendelenburg position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Narration: 'The patient will be positioned in dorsal lithotomy and then in steep Trendelenburg position'

frame_analyses.description: Front view of patient abdomen showing proposed trocar/port placement locations
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Narration: 'Ports will be placed in the following positions'

frame_analyses.description: Pelvic view with uterine manipulator visible, showing anatomical relationship
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Narration: 'A uterine manipulator is used for better mobilization'

frame_analyses.description: 3D anatomical model showing ureter identification bilaterally with clear labeling of reproductive structures
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Narration: 'The initial step is identifying the ureters bilaterally'

frame_analyses.description: Close-up showing avascular triangle region with external iliac vessels, round ligament, and infundibulopelvic ligament highlighted
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Narration: 'External iliac vessels will be identified in order to distinguish the avascular triangle. A window will be made in the avascular triangle to isolate the IP ligament from the ureter, thereby allowing safe ligation of the IP ligament'

frame_analyses.description: Anatomical illustration showing infundibulopelvic ligament and ureter relationship with surgical dissection plane
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Narration: 'The right IP ligament ligament will then be divided. The left utero-ovarian ligament, fallopian tube, and IP ligament will be divided on the right side'

frame_analyses.description: Anatomical model showing completed bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy with fallopian tubes and ovaries resected
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Narration: 'The specimens will be extracted through the umbilical port at the end of the procedure'

frame_analyses.description: Anatomical view showing final pelvic anatomy after specimen removal with emphasis on hemostasis
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Narration: 'Hemostasis will be assured prior to removal of the laparoscopic ports'

confidence_rationale: This is clearly an educational/instructional video with 3D anatomical illustrations, narrated surgical planning content, and no actual laparoscopic performance. The frames show animated diagrams, text overlays, and voiced narration explaining surgical steps, not a trainee performing an FLS task.
cannot_determine: No actual surgical instruments or trainer equipment visible
technique_summary: This video is educational content providing anatomical overview and surgical planning for laparoscopic bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. It covers patient positioning, port placement, anatomical landmarks (ureters, avascular triangle, ligaments), and specimen extraction. Not scorable as an FLS performance.
strengths: Clear anatomical labeling and identification of critical structures
Systematic coverage of surgical planning steps
Good visual demonstration of vascular anatomy and ligament relationships
Emphasis on ureteral identification and avascular triangle dissection

demonstrated_techniques: Patient positioning in dorsal lithotomy and Trendelenburg
verbal_cues: Identify ureters bilaterally as first critical step
Use uterine manipulator for better mobilization
Create window in avascular triangle to separate IP ligament from ureter
Identify external iliac vessels to define avascular triangle boundaries
common_errors_addressed: Ureteral injury prevention through early bilateral identification
Vascular injury prevention by understanding avascular triangle anatomy
Safe ligament division by proper separation of structures before ligation

improvement_suggestions: This is instructional content only - not applicable for performance scoring
